nativeRange.habitat: Subtidal coral reef zones and rubble areas; tends to grow anchored to hard substrate or rubble in moderate water movement.

imageCandidates.notes: Product image from Mosaic Macros listing for Eucheuma isiforme; sole hobby trade source for this species.

summary: A rare display-grade red macroalgae with erect, spiny branches and highly variable coloration — specimens may arrive red, orange, or yellow. One of the more challenging macroalgae to maintain; rarely offered in the hobby trade.
careNotes: Eucheuma isiforme is a red alga (Rhodophyta, family Solieriaceae) despite trade names that may suggest otherwise. Mosaic Macros describes this species as 'rarely offered due to its difficulty' and requires Next-Day Air shipping for the live arrival guarantee, indicating greater sensitivity to shipping stress than many other macroalgae. Color is highly variable — specimens may be red, orange, or yellow depending on collection location and tank conditions. As a red alga, E. isiforme must NOT be buried in sand; burying the base will cause it to melt. It is generally advised to attach it to live rock or rubble using wedging (positioning between rocks) or zip-tie/fishing line; superglue has been documented to cause base melt in some macroalgae and should be used with caution. Moderate, indirect flow is preferable — too much direct current can damage the spiny thallus. Full-spectrum LED lighting is recommended; actinic-only spectrum has been noted by Mosaic to be insufficient for more demanding species. Weekly dosing of iron, iodine, and trace micronutrients is generally advised. Target water chemistry: temperature 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, calcium 400–450 ppm, magnesium 1250–1350 ppm, nitrate 5–20 ppm, phosphate 0.02–0.1 ppm. Herbivorous tankmates — including tangs, rabbitfish, and sea urchins — have been known to consume this alga. A sibling entry, Green Spiny Seaweed (sw-algae-050, Eucheuma sp.), represents the unidentified green-coloration form of this genus sold separately by Mosaic.
breedingNotes: Aquarium propagation is typically by fragmentation — detaching healthy branch segments and allowing them to anchor to rubble or rock. Sexual reproduction (gametophyte/sporophyte alternation) has not been documented in home aquaria.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby trade source; confirmed species identity as Eucheuma isiforme, coloration variability (red/orange/yellow), care level (difficult), and shipping sensitivity requiring Next-Day Air.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Macroalgae Care Guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source. Confirmed water parameters (temp 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, Ca 400–450 ppm, Mg 1250–1350 ppm, NO3 5–20 ppm, PO4 0.02–0.1 ppm), propagation guidance (wedging preferred, zip-tie/fishing line acceptable), red-algae sand-bed melt warning, full-spectrum lighting requirement, and weekly micronutrient dosing recommendation.
